refinements.response:
```asp
% -------------------------------------------------
% Gravity – maximal reachable colour for zero cells
% -------------------------------------------------

% ---------- grid size ----------
maxRow(MR) :- MR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
maxCol(MC) :- MC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .
height(H)  :- maxRow(MR),   H = MR + 1 .
width(W)   :- maxCol(MC),   W = MC + 1 .

% ---------- zero cells ----------
zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0).

% ---------- reachability through a path of zeros ----------
% a zero cell can reach itself
reachable(R,C,R,C) :- zero(R,C).

% move down (row+1) – the cell we step onto must be zero
reachable(R,C,R1,C) :-
    R1 = R + 1,
    zero(R1-1,C),
    reachable(R,C,R1-1,C).

% move right (col+1) – the cell we step onto must be zero
reachable(R,C,R,C1) :-
    C1 = C + 1,
    zero(R,C1-1),
    reachable(R,C,R,C1-1).

% ---------- candidate coloured cells ----------
% a coloured cell (Rc,Cc,Col) can colour a zero cell (Rz,Cz) if
% there is a zero predecessor reachable from (Rz,Cz)
candidate(Rz,Cz,Col,Rc,Cc) :-
    color(Col), Col != 0,
    input(Rc,Cc,Col),
    Rc > Rz,                     % vertical step
    Rp = Rc - 1,
    zero(Rp,Cc),
    reachable(Rz,Cz,Rp,Cc).

candidate(Rz,Cz,Col,Rc,Cc) :-
    color(Col), Col != 0,
    input(Rc,Cc,Col),
    Cc > Cz,                     % horizontal step
    Cp = Cc - 1,
    zero(Rc,Cp),
    reachable(Rz,Cz,Rc,Cp).

% ---------- maximal colour for a zero cell ----------
% the colour that is the maximum over all candidates for this zero cell
maxc(R,C,Col) :-
    candidate(R,C,Col,Rc,Cc),
    Col = #max { ColTmp : candidate(R,C,ColTmp,Rc,Cc) }.

% ---------- output ----------
% keep the originally coloured cells
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% give a zero cell the maximal reachable colour (the colour that will be
% painted on this zero cell)
output(R,C,Col) :- zero(R,C), maxc(R,C,Col).

% if a zero cell has no reachable coloured cell it stays zero
output(R,C,0)  :- zero(R,C), not maxc(R,C,_).

% ---------- domain restrictions ----------
:- height(H), width(W),
   R = 0..H-1,
   C = 0..W-1,
   output(R,C,_).

% ---------- uniqueness ----------
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Gravity – maximal reachable colour for zero cells
% -------------------------------------------------

% ---------- grid size ----------
maxRow(MR) :- MR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
maxCol(MC) :- MC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .
height(H)  :- maxRow(MR),   H = MR + 1 .
width(W)   :- maxCol(MC),   W = MC + 1 .

% ---------- zero cells ----------
zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0).

% ---------- reachability through a path of zeros ----------
% a zero cell can reach itself
reachable(R,C,R,C) :- zero(R,C).

% move down (row+1) – the cell we step onto must be zero
reachable(R,C,R1,C) :-
    R1 = R + 1,
    zero(R1-1,C),
    reachable(R,C,R1-1,C).

% move right (col+1) – the cell we step onto must be zero
reachable(R,C,R,C1) :-
    C1 = C + 1,
    zero(R,C1-1),
    reachable(R,C,R,C1-1).

% ---------- candidate coloured cells ----------
% a coloured cell (Rc,Cc,Col) can colour a zero cell (Rz,Cz) if
% there is a zero predecessor reachable from (Rz,Cz)
candidate(Rz,Cz,Col,Rc,Cc) :-
    color(Col), Col != 0,
    input(Rc,Cc,Col),
    Rc > Rz,                     % vertical step
    Rp = Rc - 1,
    zero(Rp,Cc),
    reachable(Rz,Cz,Rp,Cc).

candidate(Rz,Cz,Col,Rc,Cc) :-
    color(Col), Col != 0,
    input(Rc,Cc,Col),
    Cc > Cz,                     % horizontal step
    Cp = Cc - 1,
    zero(Rc,Cp),
    reachable(Rz,Cz,Rc,Cp).

% ---------- maximal colour for a zero cell ----------
% the colour that is the maximum over all candidates for this zero cell
maxc(R,C,Col) :-
    candidate(R,C,Col,Rc,Cc),
    Col = #max { ColTmp : candidate(R,C,ColTmp,Rc,Cc) }.

% ---------- output ----------
% keep the originally coloured cells
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% give a zero cell the maximal reachable colour (the colour that will be
% painted on this zero cell)
output(R,C,Col) :- zero(R,C), maxc(R,C,Col).

% if a zero cell has no reachable coloured cell it stays zero
output(R,C,0)  :- zero(R,C), not maxc(R,C,_).

% ---------- domain restrictions ----------
:- height(H), width(W),
   R = 0..H-1,
   C = 0..W-1,
   output(R,C,_).

% ---------- uniqueness ----------
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.
